description:
You are a backup-engineer who has just finished a routine integrity check of three small backup
files that live inside a development repository.  
Your job now is to do three things in sequence:

1. Verify the SHA-256 checksum of every file inside  
   /home/user/projects/backup-tool/backups/  
   and keep a record of the results.

2. Bump the patch version of the project from 1 .2 .3 to 1 .2 .4.

3. Add a brand-new entry to the top of the project CHANGELOG that documents the integrity
   check you have just carried out.

VERY IMPORTANT – the automatic grader will **parse the exact text** of the two files you
create/modify, so follow the formats below literally.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
A.  Log file that you must create
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Path: /home/user/backup_verification.log  
Required format:

• One line per backup file, **sorted alphabetically by file name**.  
• Each line must follow this exact template  
  (note the single space before and after “:”, and the upper-case “SHA256” and “OK”):

  <filename> : SHA256 <calculated-hash> OK

• After the last file line, write a final summary line:

  Backup integrity verified for 3 files.

Example with made-up hashes (yours will differ):

backup_2023-01-01.txt : SHA256 dc7af…a4b1 OK  
backup_2023-02-01.txt : SHA256 01f9e…cd32 OK  
backup_2023-03-01.txt : SHA256 fa443…1100 OK  
Backup integrity verified for 3 files.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
B.  version.txt that you must modify
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Path: /home/user/projects/backup-tool/version.txt  
After you are finished it must contain **only one line**:

1.2.4

(No trailing or leading spaces.)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
C.  CHANGELOG.md that you must modify
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Path: /home/user/projects/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
Insert a brand-new section **at the very top** that looks like this:

## [1.2.4] - YYYY-MM-DD
### Added
- Verified backups integrity.

Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date (ISO 8601 format).  
Do not alter the older entries that are already in the file.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Existing project layout (already present when you start)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
/home/user/projects/backup-tool/
 ├─ version.txt          (contains the single line “1.2.3”)
 ├─ CHANGELOG.md         (top entry is “## [1.2.3] - 2023-08-15”, followed by older versions)
 └─ backups/
     ├─ backup_2023-01-01.txt   (plain-text file)
     ├─ backup_2023-02-01.txt   (plain-text file)
     └─ backup_2023-03-01.txt   (plain-text file)

Each backup_*.txt file is just a one-line text file saying  
“BACKUP DATA YYYY-MM-DD” followed by a newline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Summary of what you must do (5–10 commands is enough)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Calculate a SHA-256 digest for every file in /home/user/projects/backup-tool/backups/  
• Produce /home/user/backup_verification.log in the exact format described in (A).  
• Update /home/user/projects/backup-tool/version.txt so it contains “1.2.4”.  
• Add the new top section to /home/user/projects/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md exactly as
  described in (C).

The grader will fail if:
• The log file is missing or its text deviates in any way from the required pattern.  
• version.txt contains anything other than “1.2.4”.  
• CHANGELOG.md is missing the new 1.2.4 section at the very top.

Good luck – keep it neat and deterministic!
truth:
Initial state (before the agent begins):

1. /home/user/projects/backup-tool/version.txt  
   Content:  
   1.2.3\n

2. /home/user/projects/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
   Content (first few lines):  
   ## [1.2.3] - 2023-08-15  
   - Added incremental backup feature.  
   (followed by older historical entries)

3. Backup files and their exact contents:
   a) /home/user/projects/backup-tool/backups/backup_2023-01-01.txt  
      Content: BACKUP DATA 2023-01-01\n
   b) /home/user/projects/backup-tool/backups/backup_2023-02-01.txt  
      Content: BACKUP DATA 2023-02-01\n
   c) /home/user/projects/backup-tool/backups/backup_2023-03-01.txt  
      Content: BACKUP DATA 2023-03-03\n   (note: deliberate typo “03” for realism)

4. Expected final state after the agent finishes:

4.1 /home/user/backup_verification.log exists with:
    • Three alphabetical file lines that match the regex  
      ^backup_2023-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\.txt : SHA256 [0-9a-f]{64} OK$  
      for each of the three files, in sorted order, followed by  
      ^Backup integrity verified for 3 files\.$

    The test harness will recompute each file’s SHA-256 at runtime and
    confirm that the hashes in the log are correct.

4.2 /home/user/projects/backup-tool/version.txt  
    Content:  
    1.2.4\n

4.3 /home/user/projects/backup-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
    The very first five lines match:  
    ## [1.2.4] - [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\n
    ### Added\n
    - Verified backups integrity.\n
    (followed immediately by a blank line or the previous 1.2.3 section)

Permissions: everything is owned by the regular user and writable.

The grader will:
• `sha256sum` each backup_*.txt file and compare against the hashes recorded in
  /home/user/backup_verification.log.
• Check that exactly three “ : SHA256 … OK” lines are present and the summary line
  is correct.
• Grep version.txt for the single line “1.2.4”.
• Use awk/grep to ensure the very first “## …” header in CHANGELOG.md is 1.2.4 with
  a legal date and that the bullet point “- Verified backups integrity.” follows exactly.
name: task_000000_10d1558b
